summary: We are in a high-velocity week with the launch of the Intelligent China program website and the Asia Start campaign going live, while simultaneously advancing the joint 'Going Global' report with IE Business School for a September launch in Singapore.
summaryDetail: The biggest push right now is the Intelligent China program: Joseph is finalizing the website this week, and once live, the team will immediately start the SCM campaign. Concurrently, the Asia Start campaign is launching with two parallel routes (AI and traditional) for the first month, and the team is analyzing existing participant profiles to optimize targeting. On the content front, the joint 'Going Global' report with IE Business School is moving fast—Felix is meeting with Tech in Asia and Tyson this week to confirm event partnerships for the September launch, while the team begins translating the Chinese report into English and creating a one-pager proposal. Urgent this week: finalizing the homepage copy and removing Professor He Hua from the website by July 2, following up on social media agency selection (prioritizing Media Minds), and getting the Shun Tian Shu press release out with the 10 points. Coming up: the IE report translation and promotion plan, and the EU-China cooperation discussion will inform future thought leadership.

meetings.title: SEO & Program Prioritization Meeting
meetings.summary: Discussed SEO strategy to improve search ranking for programs in China, prioritizing Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships.

meetings.title: Marketing Team Meeting
meetings.summary: Team discussed website menu feedback, brochure updates, social media agency search, and IE report translation. Decisions made on Shun Tian Shu press release and agency selection.

meetings.title: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report
meetings.summary: Team discussed jointly publishing a report on Chinese companies globalizing to Southeast Asia, with a main research piece from Professor Leeway and op-eds from both schools. They plan to launch at Tech in Asia or Tyson event in Singapore in mid-September.

meetings.title: Asia Start & New Program Planning
meetings.summary: Discussed Asia Start campaign with two routes (AI vs traditional), reviewed results, and introduced a new in-depth program 'Intelligent China' targeting foreigners, with focus on module one in Beijing.

meetings.title: EU-China Cooperation Discussion
meetings.summary: Discussion on EU-China relations, focusing on geopolitical tensions, de-risking, and the role of companies versus governments in market operations.

decisions.text: Prioritize Asia Star and Intelligent China programs over US partnerships for initial SEO focus.
Intelligent China webpage to launch this week; Asia Star page already live.
Only one press release for Shun Tian Shu, incorporating 10 points.
Prioritize Media Minds as social media agency due to budget constraints.
Report will be jointly published by CKGSB and IE Business School.
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Working title: 'The New Chinese Playbook for Going Global'.
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Report will include a main research piece from Professor Leeway, op-eds from both deans, and industry perspectives (case studies or alumni op-eds).
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Target to have a digital version ready by early September for launch in Singapore.
decisions.meetingTitle: New Chinese Playbook for Going Global Report

decisions.text: Asia Start campaign will run two parallel routes for first month: AI (Joseph) and traditional (Daisy).
Intelligent China program will be marketed as a deeper dive compared to Asia Start, focusing on module one first.
Asia Start campaigns should be set to audience observation mode instead of audience targeting mode.
decisions.meetingTitle: RE: 回复: Follow-up for this week

decisions.text: The 8-chapter structure for the IE University report is approved; ~40 pages is appropriate for English audience.
Professor He Hua has been removed from the CKGSB website.
decisions.meetingTitle: RE: Faculty Departure Notice: Professor He Hua
